title: Aswan ⇄ Luxor
summary: A classic five-night Nile voyage blending awe-inspiring temples with the tranquil rhythm of river life — sailable in either direction.
description: Journey through the heart of ancient Egypt between the engineering marvels of Aswan and the monumental wonders of Luxor. Five nights allow you to explore the major highlights at a relaxed pace, with the option of a breathtaking side trip to Abu Simbel.
routeOverview: The classic stretch of the Nile, sailed at a relaxed pace. Both directions visit the same great temples — Philae, Kom Ombo, Edfu, El Kab, and the monuments of Thebes — with the order tailored to your arrival city and an optional excursion to Abu Simbel.

routes.label: Aswan → Luxor
routes.overview: Journey through the heart of ancient Egypt on a classic Nile voyage, blending awe-inspiring temple visits with the tranquil rhythm of river life — from the engineering marvels of Aswan to the monumental wonders of Luxor.

timeline.title: Aswan — Embarkation & City Highlights
timeline.description: Board in Aswan and explore the city's ancient and modern marvels.

schedule.text: Embark your dahabiya in Aswan and enjoy lunch on board.
Visit Philae Temple on Agilkia Island, the monumental High Dam that created Lake Nasser, the British-built Old Dam of 1902, and the Unfinished Obelisk in the granite quarries.
Dinner and your first night moored in Aswan.

timeline.title: Abu Simbel Option & Sailing to Kom Ombo
timeline.description: An optional dawn excursion, then north to the double temple.

schedule.text: An early excursion (by plane or convoy) to the colossal temples of Abu Simbel, built by Ramses II.
A relaxed visit to Aswan's Botanical Gardens on Kitchener's Island and a traditional Nubian village.
Begin your cruise northward.
Disembark to explore the unique Temple of Kom Ombo, dedicated to Sobek and Horus.
Moored near Edfu.

timeline.title: Edfu Temple & Onward to El Kab
timeline.description: Egypt's best-preserved temple, reached by horse-drawn carriage.

schedule.text: Travel by horse-drawn carriage to the immensely well-preserved Temple of Edfu, dedicated to the falcon god Horus.
Cruise toward the ancient site of El Kab.
Enjoy the passing river scenery from the sundeck.
Moored near El Kab.

timeline.title: El Kab & Sailing to Luxor via Esna
timeline.description: An ancient city, then through the Esna lock to Luxor.

schedule.text: Explore the archaeological site of El Kab — an ancient city of tombs, temples, and rock inscriptions.
Cruise toward Luxor, passing through the Esna Lock (observed from deck) and the town of Esna.
Arrive in Luxor.
Moored in Luxor.

timeline.title: Luxor — Thebes, City of the Gods
timeline.description: Both banks of ancient Thebes in a single, unforgettable day.

schedule.text: The Theban Necropolis — the Valley of the Kings, the Temple of Hatshepsut at Deir el-Bahari, and the Colossi of Memnon.
The magnificent Karnak Temple complex and the elegant Luxor Temple, beautifully lit at dusk.
Farewell dinner and final night on board in Luxor.
After breakfast on board, disembark at your leisure in Luxor, marking the end of your Nile cruise.

routes.label: Luxor → Aswan
routes.overview: Sail south along the legendary Nile from the world's greatest open-air museum in Luxor to the graceful city of Aswan, exploring the major highlights at a relaxed pace — concluding with the option of a breathtaking side trip to Abu Simbel.

timeline.title: Luxor — Arrival & East Bank Wonders
timeline.description: Board in Luxor amid the grandeur of ancient Thebes.

schedule.text: Board your dahabiya in Luxor and enjoy a welcome lunch.
Explore the East Bank — the vast Karnak Temple complex of pylons and obelisks, and the majestic Luxor Temple, illuminated after dusk.
Dinner and your first night moored in Luxor.

timeline.title: Luxor's West Bank & Sailing South
timeline.description: The Theban necropolis, then south through the Esna lock.

schedule.text: Cross to the West Bank necropolis — the Valley of the Kings, the terraced Temple of Hatshepsut, and the Colossi of Memnon.
Begin your cruise south, passing through the Esna Lock.
Moored near the ancient site of El Kab.

timeline.title: El Kab & The Temple of Horus at Edfu
timeline.description: A pre-dynastic city and Egypt's best-preserved temple.

schedule.text: Explore the ruins of El Kab, an important pre-dynastic and Pharaonic settlement.
Cruise to Edfu.
Travel by horse-drawn carriage to the magnificent Temple of Edfu, dedicated to the falcon god Horus.
Continue to the mooring at Gebel el-Silsila.
Moored near El Silsila.

timeline.title: Gebel el-Silsila & Kom Ombo
timeline.description: Ancient quarries and the double temple, then on to Aswan.

schedule.text: Discover Gebel el-Silsila — the ancient sandstone quarry with rock-cut shrines and royal stelae along the banks.
Cruise to Kom Ombo.
Tour the unique Temple of Kom Ombo, dedicated to both Sobek and Horus.
Enjoy the final stretch of sailing toward Aswan.
Moored in Aswan.

timeline.title: Aswan's Treasures & Abu Simbel Option
timeline.description: A full day with two ways to experience the south.

schedule.text: Visit the sublime island Temple of Philae, the High Dam, the Old Dam, and the Unfinished Obelisk.
A very early excursion (by plane or convoy) to the colossal rock-cut temples of Abu Simbel, returning to Aswan's highlights in the afternoon.
Farewell dinner and final night on board in Aswan.

timeline.title: Disembarkation
timeline.description: Farewell to the river — with Abu Simbel within reach.

schedule.text: After breakfast on board, disembark at your leisure in Aswan, marking the end of your cruise.

faq.a: Both directions visit the same temples; only the order and the sailing flow differ. Sailing from Luxor to Aswan is upriver and can feel more relaxed, and ends near Abu Simbel for an optional extension. We tailor the direction to your arrival city.
Abu Simbel is offered as an optional excursion by plane or convoy on the Aswan day. We arrange it on request and quote it with your charter.

faq.q: Are entrance fees and guides included?
faq.a: The boat, all meals on board, and the crew are included. Temple entrance fees, expert guides, and land transfers are arranged for you and quoted with your private charter.
